name: The Wild Romantic
description: Travels for the drama — crumbling castles, storm-watching from cliffside hotels, vineyards at sunset, getting lost in medieval cities. High Setting, high Atmosphere, moderate Design (prefers patina over polish). The emotional temperature of a place matters more than anything concrete. Tests whether the system can capture an aesthetic that's more about feeling than any single measurable domain.

contradictions.revealed: Actually loves one ultra-modern hotel (Juvet Landscape Hotel) because it serves the landscape rather than competing with it. The rule isn't 'no modernism' — it's 'no ego'
contradictions.expectedResolution: Properties where architecture serves nature/place, regardless of era

voiceAnswerGuidance.memorable-stays: Every description sounds like a novel opening. 'We found this crumbling palazzo on the Amalfi coast — the kind of place where the shutters don't quite close and you can hear the sea all night. The walls were this faded terra cotta and there were bougainvillea growing through the bathroom window. We drank wine on the terrace and watched a storm come in over Capri.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.anti-stay: Anything soulless or generic. 'A new-build hotel in Dubai. Everything was marble and gold leaf. It was like being inside a shopping mall. No weather, no history, no imperfection. I felt like I was nowhere.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.go-back-place: Somewhere with dramatic natural beauty and history layered together. 'The Scottish Highlands. There's a loch where I once watched the mist come down and the whole world disappeared. It felt like time didn't exist.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.cultural-engagement: Through landscape, architecture, and story. Wanders ruins, reads local history, sits in old churches. 'I want to feel the weight of a place. Who lived here, who loved here, who left.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.emotional-core: Travels to feel deeply. Seeks beauty that aches. ROMANTIC_SEEKER.
